synthesised_context: CORE PROBLEM I accidentally deleted the Microsoft Solitaire icon from my taskbar and I need to find a way to get it back. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO Operating System: Windows 8.1 Application: Microsoft Solitaire

CORE PROBLEM I recently upgraded my operating system and strongly dislike the new version of the Calculator app. I want to restore the older, smaller version of the calculator that I had before the upgrade. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO Operating System: Windows 8.1 (recently upgraded from Windows 8.0; also requesting changes for Windows 10) Application: Windows Calculator Specific Settings Used: Scientific mode SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS The new calculator is listed under my apps with "(new)" stated to the side of it. Clicking directly on the "calc" app opens this new version. The new calculator interface is big, bulky, and covers too much of the screen, making it impractical when working alongside other windows. The size issue is especially problematic when using extra functions like the Scientific settings. The original calculator was much smaller and more practical for my workflow. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED I found a workaround to reinstall the classic calculator via a YouTube tutorial, but I prefer not to rely on third-party sources to get the old app back. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT I am currently frustrated that I might have to go to the Start Screen and search for "calc" every single time just to access the version of the calculator I actually like. I want an official option or choice built into future Windows updates to let users select the classic calculator.

CORE PROBLEM The Bing News app tile on my laptop crashes immediately every time I try to open it. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO - Device: Samsung laptop (Intel Core i7 processor) - Operating System: Windows 8.1 - Application: Bing News app SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS - The app crashes immediately after opening up. - When I right-click on the saved app, absolutely nothing happens. - The Bing Sports app works perfectly fine, but the News app continues to crash. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED - I tried right-clicking on the saved app to access options, but I get no response. - I tried a previously suggested troubleshooting step; it successfully got the Sports app working, but the News app crashed again. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT - I use the Bing News app tile daily. - The issue started today (April 30, 2014) right after I updated or added some sources for content in the customize tile. - I noticed on a blog that other users are experiencing this exact same problem.

CORE PROBLEM I am unable to edit existing comments in my spreadsheet using the standard right-click menu without performing an extra step first. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO Microsoft Excel 2007 SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS When I right-click on an older, existing comment and select "Edit Comments", the program is completely unresponsive. This issue started happening within the last week. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED I tested adding new comments by clicking "Insert Comment". These newly added comments can be edited in the normal way without any issues; the problem only affects the old comments. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT I discovered a workaround: if I right-click and choose "Show/Hide Comment" first, and then go back and choose "Edit Comments", it allows me to edit the text. I want to fix this so the extra "Show/Hide" step is no longer necessary.

CORE PROBLEM When I navigate to Start > Control Panel > System and Security and click "Back up your computer", absolutely nothing happens. I cannot launch the backup utility from the Control Panel. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO OS: Windows 7 Device: HP computer Software: No third-party backup software is installed on the system. SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS Clicking the backup option in the Control Panel produces no visible response, though every other item in the Control Panel works perfectly fine. When I click "Backup and Restore", the Windows Application Log Events show "Backup 753 started", and exactly 5 minutes later it logs "Backup 754 stopped". My system came directly from HP with this problem already present out of the box, along with a few other issues. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED I verified that the Block Level Backup Engine, Volume Shadow Copy, and Windows Backup services are all set to "Started" and "Automatic". I checked the properties for all three of those services and confirmed that "Allow service to interact with desktop" is NOT checked for any of them. I ran an SFC scan, which found no problems. I performed a clean boot, but the backup utility still fails to run from the Control Panel. I opened a command window and ran `sdclt.exe /configure`. This successfully launches the process, displaying "Starting Windows Backup" followed by "Set up Backup". ADDITIONAL CONTEXT Because `sdclt.exe /configure` works perfectly from the command line, the issue seems strictly isolated to launching the backup process through the Control Panel interface.

CORE PROBLEM I cannot sign into the Skype desktop application, and Skype WiFi is failing to connect to my network. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO - Operating System: Windows 7 (64-bit) - Application: Skype for Windows Desktop - Skype Version: 7.5.0.102 (from 2015) - Browser: Internet Explorer 11 SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS - When I enter my correct username and password and click 'sign in', I receive the error message: 'Skype can't connect' and I cannot proceed any further. - Skype WiFi is unable to connect; it states that the network is not responding correctly and asks me to try connecting to a different network. - Whenever I try to update Skype to a newer version, the application crashes mid-update. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED - I have verified that my internet connection is active and working perfectly on all other programs. - I have previously attempted to update Skype to fix its outdated version, but the update process always crashes. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT - I have changed my physical location since the last time I successfully signed into Skype. - I am unsure if the sign-in error and the Skype WiFi network error are connected.

CORE PROBLEM My Windows Settings, Steam, and the Microsoft Store app will not display on my screen when I open them. The applications appear to be running but are completely invisible and inaccessible. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO OS: Windows 10 Software: Steam, Microsoft Store app, Microsoft Bingo Hardware: External hard drive (used for Steam data), newly installed RAM cards SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS For around a month, my Settings disappear whenever I open them. The app still shows on the taskbar, and I can see the preview tab when I hover my mouse over it, but clicking it does nothing. Recently, Steam started doing the exact same thing. Because of this, I can no longer play any Steam games unless I already created a shortcut for them. I also cannot open my Microsoft Store app by clicking on the shopping-bag icon, which is preventing me from reinstalling Microsoft Bingo for free. This is my first time experiencing this issue. I am not getting any error messages at all. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED I shut down and restarted my computer. I reset the memory. I closed all running programs using the Task Manager. I have specifically avoided reinstalling Steam because I do not want to lose all my data stored on my external hard drive. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT The only changes I made to my system prior to this issue starting were installing new RAM cards, installing a couple of Windows updates, and installing Steam along with a couple of Steam games.

CORE PROBLEM When I right-click and select "copy" on YouTube, the webpage reloads instead of copying the content. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO Operating System: Windows 10 Browser: Microsoft Edge Website: YouTube SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS Every time I right-click and press copy on YouTube, the page immediately reloads. The issue keeps happening repeatedly every time I try to copy. I have no idea when this started or what caused it. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED I tried right-clicking and pressing copy multiple times, but it does the exact same thing every time. I tested a different browser, and the issue does not happen there. I tested different webpages, and the issue does not happen on them. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT The problem is completely isolated to YouTube while using Microsoft Edge.

CORE PROBLEM I am using a VLOOKUP function in Excel to match deduplicated timestamp values from a second table to my original table, but it is returning incorrect values for certain timestamps. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO Microsoft Excel SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS My original table has a column of timestamps (Column A) and a column for the VLOOKUP return values (Column B). The timestamp cells are configured as "custom h:mm". I created a second table with deduplicated values of the timestamps to use as my lookup array. When I run the VLOOKUP, some values return correctly (for example, 0:11 returns 11, 2:05 returns 29, and 6:06 returns 78). However, other values return incorrectly (for example, 2:06 returns 15 instead of the expected 30, and 3:05 returns 14 instead of the expected 41). WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED I tried using the VALUE function. I tried changing the columns to another data format other than time. I copied the data to another table. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT The source of the original timestamps is a very old external software. There is no way to reimport the data differently.

CORE PROBLEM I created a Hotmail account because I was promised a free $3 Amazon gift card, but I never received the gift card. CORE PROBLEM My JavaScript code for a web notification's click event is failing to bring the Microsoft Edge browser window to the front when it is minimized or behind other active windows. The `window.focus()` and `window.open()` methods inside the `onclick` event listener are triggering, but they do not bring the Edge window to the top. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO * Operating System: Windows 10 Pro, Version 1703 (OS Build: 15063.296) * Browser: Microsoft Edge 40.15063.0.0 * Browser Engine: Microsoft EdgeHTML 15.15063 * Libraries: jQuery 3.1.1 (`jquery-3.1.1.min.js`) * Previous Working Browser Version: Microsoft Edge 38.xxxx SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS * The `onclick` action itself is successfully getting triggered, so the issue is not that the click is unresponsive. * When I minimize the browser window after triggering the notification and then click on the notification popup, the Edge window remains minimized/behind other apps instead of coming to the foreground. * This issue started occurring immediately after the latest Edge update; the exact same code worked perfectly in Edge version 38.xxxx. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED * I tested the exact same code in Google Chrome. When I minimize Chrome and click the notification, the Chrome window is successfully taken to the front. * I created a standalone HTML and JS example that can be run on any server to isolate the issue. * I created a JSFiddle to demonstrate the problem: https://jsfiddle.net/jintujsfiddle/7qbn56f0/ ADDITIONAL CONTEXT * The issue is isolated to Edge; it works in all other browsers that support web notifications. * The specific JavaScript code I am using inside the notification click event is: ```javascript notification.onclick = function(event) { parent.focus(); window.focus(); window.open().close(); this.close(); } ``` * My full standalone HTML setup includes a button that checks if `'Notification' in window`, requests permission, and generates a new notification with a body (`'You have a new message.'`) and a custom icon (`'https://s3-us-west-2.amazonaws.com/s.cdpn.io/515428/skype_icon.png'`).

CORE PROBLEM I have a spreadsheet with 100 rows of data, but I am unable to sort more than 48 rows at a time. Attempting to sort 49 rows or more causes the sort to fail or Excel to crash. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO Software: Microsoft Excel (Office 365). My previous version of Excel before 365 appeared to work fine. Hardware: 8GB of memory installed. SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS Because of header rows, I am trying to sort rows 3 to 102. Rows 3 through 51 (48 rows) will sort successfully. When I add row 52 (49 rows), it will no longer sort. When I apply a filter and try to sort 49 rows, Excel crashes and restarts. The built-in sort in the filter does not expand to try to sort all the columns. When I use the sort function in the Data tab at the top of the page, Excel crashes. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED I tried using the built-in sort in the filter, but it didn't work. I tried using the sort in the Data tab, which resulted in a crash. I used Ctrl+End to check for extra unseen columns or rows; it correctly takes me to the bottom right corner of my data (and the bottom right corner of my lookup range), so there are no hidden extra rows/columns. I made a copy of the spreadsheet and erased all lookup and formula cells, but I still can only sort 48 rows. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT The total sheet is 40 columns by 107 rows. I have one column that does VLOOKUPs (100 cells), but the lookup range is only 2 columns by 17 rows. I also have three columns of totals/max lookups, which is 300 cells of formulas.

CORE PROBLEM In Office 2016 for Mac, the drop-down menus (ComboBoxes) in my VBA Userforms are failing because the Userform renders in front of the ComboBox items, making them impossible to use. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO * Operating System: Mac * Software: Microsoft Office 2016 * Affected Applications: Word 15.38 and Excel 15.38 * Environment: Enterprise network with 400+ Mac machines * Other: I currently run a Windows Virtual Machine (VM) specifically for Userform editing SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS * When clicking a ComboBox in a macro, the drop-down list appears behind the Userform instead of in front of it. * This is happening on the standard production build. * The issue started recently following an update; these exact ComboBoxes were already working perfectly in earlier builds of Office 2016. * I have screenshots comparing the correct behavior in Office 2011 to the broken behavior in the current 2016 build. * Because this breaks the actual user interface, our decision-makers literally cannot use macros that have been in place for years. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED * I ruled out rolling back via Time Machine, as that is not a viable option for 400+ machines. * I reported the issue to our system administrators so they can prepare for a network-wide roll-back to a previous version if necessary. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT * This is a massive issue for us and has our helpdesk phones ringing constantly. * I am not the system admin, so I do not know the exact software distribution method they use to deploy Office to our machines. * I am currently forced to run a whole Windows OS in a VM just for Userform editing because the feature is missing on the Mac side, but my goal is to eventually run a Mac-only setup without that overhead.

CORE PROBLEM I want to change the font styles and sizes used in the Navigation Pane and the Comment balloons in Word, but there is no obvious way to do this from the standard menus. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO Microsoft Word 2016 for Mac SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS There are no settings or options available directly within the Navigation Pane or the Review menu to adjust the font sizes for the pane or the comment balloons. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED I searched through the Navigation Pane and the Review menu for font settings. I also contacted several levels of Microsoft Support, but they were unable to help me make it work. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT My name is David. After hanging up with support, I kept experimenting and discovered a workaround that I would like others to validate, as it seems to work every time for me: - For the Navigation Pane: I open the pane, select Format/Styles from the Word menu, choose the "Document Map" style from the pull-down menu, adjust the fonts/sizes, and click Apply. - For Comment Balloons: I display the balloons, highlight text from any comment, select Format/Styles from the Word menu, choose the "Comment Text" style, make my changes, and click Apply.

CORE PROBLEM The Microsoft Calculator app is running in the background, slowing down my computer, and hogging a massive amount of memory. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO OS: Windows 10 Software: Microsoft Calculator (specifically the actual calculator app, not a spreadsheet program) SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS The app runs continuously in the background and uses heaps of memory, which slows everything else down. I noticed that this does not seem to be the same version of the calculator that I previously had on Windows 7. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED I have been repeatedly going into Task Manager ("taskmaster") to manually kill the process. I also located the calculator under my Apps settings and reset it.

CORE PROBLEM I installed the Bing desktop app, but when I type a search into the taskbar and press Enter or click the search icon, absolutely nothing happens. It fails to open a web browser to display the search results. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO * Operating System: Windows 7 (receives automatic updates) * Software: Bing desktop app * Browsers Installed: Internet Explorer 11 (update kb4012204) and Google Chrome SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS * When I type in the Bing taskbar, the dropdown boxes work and it successfully shows search suggestions. * Pressing Enter or clicking the search icon does nothing. * On my past computers, doing this would always automatically open Internet Explorer. * I checked the Bing app settings, but there is no option available to configure opening a browser. * I suspect my system might be infected with malware or a rootkit because I recently saw a fake "Urgent firefox update" page. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED * Removed and reinstalled the Bing desktop app twice. * Ran Avast and Malwarebytes Anti-Malware; both found nothing. * Reinstalled Avast and Malwarebytes and ran them both a second time; still no infections found. * Ran the MS Safety Scanner for 3 hours; found nothing. * Ran Windows Defender; found nothing. * Ran a trial version of an unnamed paid anti-malware program. It claimed to find several issues and a possible rootkit, but I did not purchase it because I am wary it might be a fake program. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT * I am unsure how the Bing app determines which browser to open since I have both Chrome and IE installed, or if it is designed to only open IE. * I am wondering if the Bing desktop app is simply incompatible with Internet Explorer on Windows 7.

CORE PROBLEM I am unable to search for videos using Bing. Whenever I try to use the Bing Video search, I get stuck in a redirect loop that sends me back to the default Bing homepage instead of displaying video results. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO Browser: Firefox (I stopped using Internet Explorer two years ago because it was causing PC issues) Operating System: Currently on an older version of Windows, but upgrading to Windows 10 in a few days Security Software: Avast Antivirus and Windows Defender SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS The issue started 3 or 4 days ago. Bing searches for Web, Image, Maps, News, and Explore work perfectly. When I click "videos" from my usual Bing search template, I am redirected to the default Bing search page (which displays a picture of flying birds and random unrelated videos at the bottom). If I type a subject like "grumpy cat" on that default page, nothing happens. If I type "grumpy cat videos", it takes me to the regular web search results. However, if I click "videos" from those results, it loops me right back to the default Bing homepage. If I search "cat videos" from the web header and click on one of the selections, I am once again redirected back to the default homepage picture. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED A few days ago, I ran a disk scan at boot-up. I ran a full virus scan with Avast. I checked Windows Defender (no threats were found by either security program). I attempted to follow some advanced troubleshooting steps but stopped because I messed up and was not at a comfort level to continue. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT I have used Bing for videos thousands of times previously without any issues. My current workaround is to switch to Google to search for videos or to go directly to YouTube using my bookmarks.

CORE PROBLEM I want PowerPoint to stop prompting me to open files from OneDrive and just open my local files. I cannot adjust the OneDrive settings to fix this because I am completely locked out of my Live.com account. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO PowerPoint for Mac version 15.33 SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS Every time I try to open a file in PowerPoint, a box appears showing both my local drive and OneDrive. OneDrive is always highlighted by default, and a dialogue box prompts me to log in. When I attempt to log into Live.com, I receive the exact error message: "Your account or password is incorrect. If you don't remember your password please reset." WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED To open my files, I currently have to click cancel on the OneDrive login prompt, close out an error message, and manually switch the view to my local drive. To fix the account issue, I tried resetting my Live.com password last week. The system will not accept the new password or the old password, trapping me in a continuous password reset loop. I tried getting help from the Microsoft chatbot, but it only told me to reset my password again. I eventually bypassed the login issue by creating and logging into Live.com with a completely new username. I also posted a question about the account login issue to the support forum but have not received an answer. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT I do not use OneDrive at all. I cannot call Microsoft support because there is no help number provided. I need to combine my new and old Live.com accounts (or delete one), but I cannot do that without being able to log into the old account first.

CORE PROBLEM I need a way to automatically highlight the minimum, maximum, and mean values directly within a source data column so I can easily locate them. I currently calculate and display the min, max, and mean of a particular column. CORE PROBLEM PowerPoint crashed while I was typing, and now my presentation file is completely corrupted and will not open. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO OS: Mac (Primary), Windows (used for troubleshooting) Software: Office Mac 365 (PPTX365) Version: 15.37.0 (17080500) SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS The specific presentation file is corrupted and cannot be opened. The file size still shows as 80MB, indicating the data is still there. The crash occurred about 6 minutes after a manual save, destroying the file. PowerPoint itself is currently working fine and does not crash; the issue is isolated to this single file. The auto-recovery file for this presentation is missing from the recovery directory, even though other recovery files are present. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED Renamed the file extension to .ppt. Updated Office Mac 365 to the newest release (15.37.0 (17080500)). Checked the auto-recovery directory for a backup. Moved the file to a Windows machine to try opening it there. Downloaded and ran a tool called powerpointrepair.net on Windows. Used two other PPT file repair software packages on Windows (both claimed they worked, but PowerPoint still could not open the resulting files). ADDITIONAL CONTEXT I manually save my work using Ctrl+S every 5 minutes, but still lost an hour of work due to the file destruction. As a workaround, I was able to change the file extension to .zip, look inside, and extract the XML of my speaker notes, though it was a painful process.

CORE PROBLEM Bing Maps no longer accepts x and y coordinates from my database to display map locations, and appears to only accept Longitude and Latitude now. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO * Service: Bing Maps * Database: MS SQL DB * Language: Classic ASP SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS * When I use my URL string with x and y coordinates, it no longer shows the specific location. Instead, it just loads the default UK Bing Maps page. * The issue started in November when Bing apparently changed their system. * An example of a URL that used to work but now fails is: `http://www.bing.com/maps/default.aspx?cp=&x=240350&y=101150&scale=5000` WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED * I have fiddled with my URL string to try and format it differently, but nothing gets the correct result to display on the map. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT * This exact setup worked perfectly for over 10 years before breaking. * My Classic ASP page pulls the coordinates from an MS SQL database. * The X and Y columns in my database hold coordinates for about 80 different links. * The specific code I use to build the URL is: `aerialStringURL2 = "http://www.bing.com/maps/default.aspx?"` `aerialStringURL2 = aerialStringURL2 & "cp=" & "&x=" & x & "&y=" & y & "&scale=5000"` * It is simple enough for me to change the zoom/scale parameter, but the core issue is strictly with the x and y parameters being ignored.

CORE PROBLEM I am experiencing frequent app crashes in PowerPoint for Mac when switching between Normal, Notes, and Outline views. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO - Application: PowerPoint 2016 for Mac (via Office 365 subscription) - Version: 15.34 (latest version) - Operating Systems: OS X 10.11.6 (El Capitan) and macOS Sierra - Hardware: Mac with 16 GB RAM - Display: Multiple monitors (though the issue also occurs on a single monitor) SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS - The application crashes when switching between Normal, Notes, and Outline views. - Switching between Notes and Normal view is the most frequent culprit, particularly when there is text in the Notes pane/field. - The crash does not happen consistently on the very first try; however, clicking occasionally on slides and switching between views will eventually force the app to crash. - The issue is not file-specific. It happens when opening a completely blank presentation as well as when using existing presentations. - I am not experiencing any font issues. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED - I applied the latest updates to Office. - I tested the issue on multiple devices and successfully reproduced the exact same crash on two Macs running El Capitan and one Mac running Sierra. - I tested the setup with and without an external display plugged in, confirming the crash occurs regardless of the monitor configuration. - I verified that this issue does not occur in PowerPoint 2011 for Mac. - I recorded a video of the issue occurring and shared it via private message. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT - I cannot share the specific presentation file I was working on due to confidentiality issues, but the bug is easily reproducible without it. - I am unsure if this same issue occurred in previous builds of PowerPoint 2016.
